name: W. C. Handy
abstract: William Christopher Handy (November 16, 1873 – March 28, 1958) was an American composer and musician who referred to himself as the Father of the Blues. He was one of the most influential songwriters in the United States.

works_created.t: I've Heard of a City Called Heaven
Boogie Woogie on St. Louis Blues

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: And I would walk out and look at the house, you know. And I just said, 'Well, at least I saw the house where he was born in Florence, Alabama.'

about_quotes.speaker: Barney Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: And, like W.C. Handy, you know. And then, that's what's going to happen. You, you can't, uh, put the man down, period.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I met him once in Montgomery. They did a concert there and that was the thrill of my life.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: The man that captured it was W.C. Handy, but it didn't belong to Handy.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Because I had heard about W.C. Handy, and it was nice to see these things as a kid.

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And then “St. Louis Blues”. “St Louis Blues,” W.C. Handy. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, well you have to give those guys that kind of respect because the St. Louis Blues- and I had been to St. Louis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
